description: One-pass review of crate secretx-keyring (/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretsx/secretx-keyring). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Scope:  — files under /home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretsx/secretx-keyring/ only. Crate version 0.3.1. Compiles clean (cargo build, cargo clippy --all-features, cargo clippy -W pedantic -W nursery — zero warnings against keyring sources). All 8 unit tests pass; integration test passes with SECRETX_KEYRING_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1 (kernel keyring available). Reviewed against dependency contract from secretx-core/src/lib.rs and compared with sibling crate secretx-desktop (near-twin, different keyring features) and secretx-systemd.
Pass 1 (my-oss) complete. Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — two P0 documentation/correctness bugs (false reboot-survival claim, silent persistent-keyring fallback) plus a non-Linux mock-fallback footgun. Code itself is clean and idiomatic; the issues are in the contract this crate advertises vs. what its dependency actually delivers.
Correctness confidence: 85% chance no further missed bug. Least confident flags: (a) finding #2 (silent persistent-keyring fallback) — I have not actually reproduced this on a system without CONFIG_PERSISTENT_KEYRINGS to verify the silent-fallback path behaves as the keyring crate's source code suggests. (b) Whether keyring::Error::Ambiguous variant could theoretically leak via Display through source: e.into() — I traced the keyutils Credential Debug impl (does NOT include the secret) but did not exhaustively trace all platform Credential Debug impls because only the linux/keyutils path is reachable from this crate's feature flags. Uncertainty driven by: no live reproduction in restricted environments; relies on reading the keyring 3.6.3 source. Would raise confidence with: a docker container without CONFIG_PERSISTENT_KEYRINGS, or with a strace-based test of put/get under those conditions.
Pass 2 (rusty) complete. Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — code reads cleanly, idiomatic spawn_blocking pattern, correct error-type bridging, no unsafe, no panics in production paths, no gratuitous clones (the per-call String clones are necessary for spawn_blocking move semantics, not borrow-checker giveup), no questionable trait impls. The only meaningful idiom finding is missing #[derive(Debug)] (#20). Remaining idiom findings are clippy-tier or opinion-tier — see #18 (full-path imports), #19 (let-else over verbose match), #22 (redundant dev-dep tokio), #21 (opinion: Send+Sync compile assertion). Clippy reports zero warnings against keyring sources at default + pedantic + nursery levels.
Rust idiom confidence: 90% chance no missed issue. Least confident flags: none. The crate has a small surface area (336 lines, one impl) and the idiom space is well-explored by clippy (zero warnings). Uncertainty driven by: nothing material — the code does what it does plainly. I am running out of real idiom complaints; the findings I filed are the only ones with engineering basis. Bikeshedding-tier suggestions (rename 'tests' module, reorder use statements, etc.) intentionally NOT filed.
title: Review: secretx-keyring rusty pass
